description: Transient amplification analysis of fitted VAR(1) operators. Tests whether operator geometry (EP score) predicts transient energy amplification (Kreiss constant, amplification ratio) in the same fitted operators. Includes phase-randomized surrogate null model to distinguish genuine non-normal amplification from fitting artifacts. All results are properties of the fitted estimator, not direct measurements of neural amplification.
guardrail: Transient amplification is computed from FITTED VAR(1) operators. It reflects a property of the estimator output, not a direct measurement of neural circuit amplification. The Kreiss constant and amplification ratio depend on preprocessing, regularization, window length, and PCA dimension. The binary 'has_hump' indicator is permissive — use hump_magnitude and surrogate comparisons for quantitative claims. Hump fraction ~1.0 may reflect estimator non-normality rather than neural amplification; the surrogate control establishes the expected baseline.
